### Post by danwood76 on 2008-06-16
Its the nvidia-xconfig (as it says at the top ;))

It looks like its messed up the screen section, you could try to manually setting the screen resolutions or just deleting the mode line all together.
My xorg.conf has no mode lines in the monitor section and works at the correct resolution.

So change that first section to be:
```

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Device "Configured Video Device"
Monitor "Configured Monitor"
Option "NoLogo" "True"
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
EndSection

```
And also the 'configured monitor' section to be:
```

Section "Monitor"
Identifier "Configured Monitor"
Vendorname "Plug 'n' Play"
Modelname "Plug 'n' Play"
Gamma 1
EndSection

```

---

   my monitor is an hitachi ACCUVUE  HM-4521D

### Post by knutschr on 2008-06-16
I don't think it is your monitor that is casinng difficulties.

Install envy-ng for gnome (the one for kde has an error) and let the program install the nvidia driver and do the setup for xorg.conf for you.

I did that whith my ATI Radeon X600 card, and at last direct rendering was working too :-)

---
